US 30-year Treasury yield hits 2007 high, pressuring risk assets –
Link
The US 30-year Treasury auction yield reached 5.06%, the highest since 2007, pushing long-term yields above 5%. Rising risk-free rates raise the discount rate for speculative assets like Bitcoin.
Matters because:
Higher yields create structural headwinds for crypto and other risk assets in the near term.

ZILLIQA: A CERTAIN AMOUNT of $ZIL WAS STOLEN FROM A COLD WALLET AT AN EXCHANGE
TEAM REPORTS SECURITY BREACH; DEPOSITS AND WITHDRAWALS TEMPORARILY PAUSED ACROSS CENTRALIZED EXCHANGES
SOURCE
TEAM

tweet.text:
We have been made aware of a security incident involving one of our exchange partners, in which ZIL was stolen from a cold wallet.

The incident is under active investigation, and we are working with the relevant parties to establish the root cause and full scope. As a precaution, all exchanges have been notified and asked to temporarily pause ZIL deposits and withdrawals to prevent the stolen funds from being moved or sold through centralised platforms.

We understand the community will have questions. We will share further updates as soon as we have verified information. Please rely only on official Zilliqa channels for updates.

Source:

Crypto's Next Billion Users Won't Buy Bitcoin First, They'll Trade the World's Biggest Moments, and that's Exactly the Point.
Source:
https://x.com/laniyiiii/status/2078915795925585983

tweet.text:
The World Cup may have done more for crypto adoption than a decade of crypto marketing.

When the tournament kicked off, prediction markets surged to a record $10.8B in weekly trading volume. June alone reached $52.8B in notional volume.

Sports have now surpassed politics as the largest category in prediction markets. In May alone, Kalshi processed more than $10B in sports contracts, roughly 60× its election volume.

But the real story isn't the volume, it's who is trading. Millions of football fans are settling World Cup bets in USDC, on blockchain rails, without ever thinking of themselves as crypto users.

Crypto's next billion users won't buy Bitcoin first.

They'll trade the World Cup final, and that's exactly the point.

recent_posts.text:
“ENTREPRENEUR DAY TRADING” - GEN Z ENTREPRENEURS INCREASINGLY FAVOR SHORT-TERM ASYMMETRIC ARBITRAGE PLAYS OVER COMMITTING TO LONG-TERM COMPANY BUILDING AS AI LOWERS THE COST OF PLACING MULTIPLE BETS
SOURCE

tweet.text:
Gen Z entrepreneurship increasingly looks like running a portfolio of asymmetric coups rather than betting everything on one serious, long-term project.

One month it’s an AI wrapper. Then a TikTok Shop product, a viral food trend, or buying scarce inventory during a shortage and renting it back at triple the price. In Europe you can also trade the state: find the latest subsidy, regulation or public failure and put yourself in the middle.

Build the website over a weekend, extract as much as possible while the window is open, then abandon it as soon as the trade stops working.

It’s basically entrepreneurial day trading. Low commitment, limited downside, permanent optionality and a small chance one bet makes you rich.

And honestly, it’s rational. Why spend ten years committing to one difficult company when AI lets you place ten cheap bets per year and one winning bet can make you rich?

Kimi Pauses New Subscriptions as Demand Pushes GPU Capacity to Its Limits
> Kimi has temporarily paused new subscriptions after surging demand pushed its GPU capacity to the limit, while it adds more compute and restructures its membership plans.
Source:
https://x.com/Kimi_Moonshot/status/2078855608565207130

tweet.text:
Kimi K3 has received far more love than we expected, and our GPUs are feeling it.

Over the past 48 hours, demand has pushed close to the limits of our current capacity. To protect the experience of existing subscribers, we're temporarily pausing new subscriptions and prioritizing compute for current members. Existing subscribed users are not affected.

We're adding capacity as fast as we can and will reopen new subscription spots in batches.

Going forward, we'll also split membership into two more focused plans: Kimi Membership for Kimi Web, App, and Work; and Kimi Code Membership for coding workflows. This will help us match compute more precisely and keep the experience stable.

Thank you for your patience and understanding!

Fed official Logan calls for interest rate hikes –
Link
Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan said the central bank should raise rates to fight elevated inflation and suggested she may vote against holding rates steady at the July meeting. She said June CPI is improving, but the path to 2% remains fragile and a modest hike would better balance risks.
Matters because:
This is the first hawkish Fed signal in weeks and could pressure risk assets if rate expectations shift.

SOURCE

DTCC tokenized markets pilot shows institutional readiness –
Link
The DTCC ran a day-long pilot on July 15 with 25+ major institutions, including JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, BlackRock, and Vanguard. It successfully tokenized equities, Treasuries, and ETFs on blockchain, with a scalable launch targeted for October 2026.
Matters because:
The trial shows legacy systems and blockchain can interoperate at institutional scale, which could accelerate RWA adoption in traditional finance.

tweet.text:
Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation’s test of tokenized markets marks a significant step toward integrating blockchain technology into traditional finance.

As the central post-trade infrastructure for U.S. markets, DTCC processed $4.7 quadrillion in securities transactions last year and holds custody of roughly $114 trillion in assets through its Depository Trust Company subsidiary. 

On July 15, 2026, it conducted a day-long pilot with more than 25 major institutions—including JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, BlackRock, Vanguard, State Street, and the NYSE operator ICE, alongside digital-asset firms like Ondo and Securitize—to demonstrate tokenized versions of real-world assets operating across collateral, repo, equities, margin, and transfers.

The trial converted assets such as Microsoft shares, Invesco QQQ, SPDR S&P 500 ETF, short-term Treasury ETFs, and various U.S. Treasuries into blockchain tokens, proving interoperability between legacy systems and distributed ledger technology.

Wall Street banks launch coordinated blockchain network for tokenized deposits to compete with stablecoins –
Link
JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo are building a shared blockchain network through The Clearing House for tokenized bank deposits. The launch is planned for H1 2027, as stablecoin transaction volumes hit a record $28 trillion in Q1 2026.
Matters because:
Traditional finance is moving directly into blockchain rails to match stablecoins on speed and programmability while keeping regulatory advantages.

tweet.text:
Wall Street banks are mounting a coordinated defense against the rapid rise of stablecoins by reviving a familiar collaborative strategy. 

Major institutions including JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo are developing a shared blockchain network for tokenized bank deposits through The Clearing House, with a planned launch in the first half of 2027. 

The effort draws directly from the model used to create Zelle for instant payments and seeks to deliver faster, 24/7 settlement while keeping funds inside the regulated banking system with its built-in protections. 

HSBC and other global players are also exploring similar infrastructure, signaling a broader industry push to integrate blockchain technology without surrendering control of core deposit and payments business to crypto issuers. Stablecoins have grown dramatically in scale and utility. 

Their total market capitalization now stands at roughly $310–315 billion, led by Tether’s USDT at around $180–187 bi
